repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Supersedes #19413 as the original contributor seems inactive.

Closes #18781.

comment_data.body:
The asterisks are the default impl, right?

```suggestion
---               (default: false) If `true`, user input is hidden (displayed as asterisks "*" by default) instead of echoed.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -71,6 +71,11 @@ end
 ---     - highlight (function)
 ---               Function that will be used for highlighting
 ---               user inputs.
+---     - secret (boolean|nil)
+---               If `true`, the user's input will be shown as
+---               a series of asterisks ("*") (defaults to `false`).

comment_data.body:
This is from the `inputsecret` docs so it seems like the asterisks can't be changed:
```
a) the user's response will be displayed as a sequence of asterisks ("*") thereby keeping the entry secret, and
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -71,6 +71,11 @@ end
 ---     - highlight (function)
 ---               Function that will be used for highlighting
 ---               user inputs.
+---     - secret (boolean|nil)
+---               If `true`, the user's input will be shown as
+---               a series of asterisks ("*") (defaults to `false`).

comment_regular_data.body: Why is this a breaking change?
@lewis6991 I'm not sure if it's breaking, but it reuses `vim.ui.input` and implementations should be adjusted to handle that.
I messed up something during the rebase, will readd the commit

In my opinion (though I have no authority here) it's not breaking; if you're passing in currently-ignored parameters into a function, you're asking for trouble. It''s also hardly a hard-to-track-down bug for anyone doing strange things with their function parameters.

Maybe the core Neovim maintainers are more cautious, however.

But once this gets merged these parameters won't be ignored anymore, and that is the problem. `vim.ui.*` functions can be replaced, and if you have your own implementation then you should add handling for this new option too.

Okay, I have negligible knowledge with respect to Vim or Neovim. But I needed the same functionality as asked in this PR, from `vim.ui.inputsecret` function. And I got that functionality from the same function but in another path `vim.fn.inputsecret`. 

**Notice, I've used `vim.fn` path, rather than ~`vim.ui`~.**  
For more info, you can run command **`:help inputsecret`**

So, is the purpose of this PR fulfilled?

I think there actually is a difference
if you look at the helpfiles there also is a `vim.fn.input()`

@PreetamSing This PR uses `vim.fn.inputsecret`, that's not the point. `vim.fn` cannot be customized, you can't (shouldn't) replace those functions with your own implementation. `vim.ui` exists so you can customize the UI.
@justinmk I updated the docs and added a test
if this just adds another field to `opts` it isn't a breaking change though right?

@max397574 This was first brought up here https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/19413#issuecomment-1193927300 (also see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/20053#issuecomment-1368266050). I'm not sure on this myself so I just left it as a breaking change unless someone objects).

title: feat(lua)!: add.vim.ui.inputsecret equivalent
